title: 2 Bedroom Apartments for Rent in Ohio City
metaTitle: 2 Bedroom Apartments for Rent in Ohio City, OH
metaDescription: 2 bedroom apartments for rent in Ohio City, Cleveland near the West Side Market and RTA Red Line. Rent ranges and voucher-friendly homes. Book a showing.
primaryKeyword: 2 bedroom apartments for rent ohio city
secondaryKeywords: two bedroom rentals near west side market
answerBox: Ohio City, in Cleveland's near-west side ZIP 44113, has 2-bedroom apartments split from converted Victorian-era rowhouses and duplexes near the West Side Market and the RTA Red Line's Ohio City station. Citywide 2-bedroom rents run about $1,100 to $1,818 a month (Zumper/RentCafe, July 2026), and we work with voucher-friendly homes in the area.

body: Ohio City is a near-west side Cleveland neighborhood in **ZIP code 44113**, directly across the Cuyahoga River from downtown, home to the historic **West Side Market** and served by GCRTA's **Red Line at the Ohio City station**. The neighborhood's housing stock is dominated by late-1800s Victorian-era houses, rowhouses, and duplexes, and a 2-bedroom layout is one of the most common results when these older buildings were carved into separate rental units — often with the higher ceilings, front porches, and full-size room proportions typical of that era, rather than a purpose-built modern floor plan.

A 2-bedroom in Ohio City works for roommates splitting rent, a household that wants a home office or guest room, or anyone who wants more space than a studio or 1-bedroom without leaving the neighborhood's walk-to-the-market convenience. We work with voucher-friendly rental homes in and around Ohio City — [book a free showing](/p/book-showing) to see current 2-bedroom availability.

sections.h2: How much does a 2-bedroom apartment cost in Ohio City?
body: Citywide, Zumper's July 4, 2026 report shows a median 2-bedroom rent of **$1,100/month** — notably close to the 1-bedroom median, which reflects the mix of smaller, older 2-bedroom conversions currently listed rather than a pricing error. RentCafe (Yardi)'s July 2, 2026 report, which weights toward larger professionally managed apartment communities averaging roughly 1,027 square feet for a 2-bedroom, shows a considerably higher citywide average of **$1,818/month**. In practice, an Ohio City 2-bedroom in an older rowhouse conversion often prices closer to the Zumper figure, while a unit in a newer managed building near the market district can run well above it.

sections.h2: Getting around from a 2-bedroom in Ohio City
body: Ohio City's own rapid-transit stop, the **Ohio City station on RTA's Red Line**, connects to downtown Tower City in one direction and toward Cleveland Hopkins Airport and the east side, including University Circle, in the other — a useful option for a household splitting a 2-bedroom who doesn't want two cars and two parking spots. West 25th Street is the neighborhood's main commercial spine, running past the West Side Market and Market Square Park, with I-90 access nearby for anyone driving.

items: **ZIP code:** 44113, near-west side, across the river from downtown
**Transit:** RTA Red Line, Ohio City station; I-90 access nearby
**Landmarks:** West Side Market, Market Square Park, W. 25th Street corridor
**Housing stock:** Late-1800s houses, rowhouses, and duplexes converted into 2-bedroom units, plus newer construction

sections.h2: Section 8 vouchers for a 2-bedroom in Ohio City
body: The **Cuyahoga Metropolitan Housing Authority (CMHA)** administers the Housing Choice Voucher (Section 8) program countywide, including Ohio City, through a year-round preliminary application and mini-lottery selection. Ohio has no statewide source-of-income law, and Cleveland has not enacted its own ordinance requiring landlords to accept vouchers, so acceptance for any specific 2-bedroom unit still depends on the individual landlord. Every rental home we work with in and around Ohio City accepts Section 8 vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready — see our [Cleveland Section 8 guide](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/) for how the CMHA process works.

sections.h2: Finding a 2-bedroom apartment in Ohio City
body: [Book a free showing](/p/book-showing) so our leasing team can walk you through current 2-bedroom availability in and around Ohio City. If you'd rather keep costs lower with less space, see our [1-bedroom Ohio City listings](/cleveland-rentals/apartments/1-bedroom-apartments-for-rent-ohio-city-cleveland/), or browse [apartments across Cleveland by neighborhood and budget](/apartments-for-rent-cleveland-oh/) for other areas. Once you've picked a home, [apply online](/apply) to move forward.

Our application process reviews income relative to rent, rental history, and any voucher or co-signer support you can bring — the same process for a 2-bedroom in Ohio City as anywhere else in our available homes. If you're splitting the unit with a roommate, having both applicants' pay stubs and references ready at the showing can speed up a decision.

faq.q: Does Rent Finder Cleveland works with 2-bedroom apartments in Ohio City?
faq.a: Yes. We work with voucher-friendly rental homes in and around Ohio City as part of our broader Cleveland-area selection. Book a free showing to see current 2-bedroom availability, since specific units change regularly.

faq.q: How much does a 2-bedroom apartment cost in Ohio City?
faq.a: Citywide, Zumper's July 2026 report puts the median 2-bedroom rent at about $1,100/month, while RentCafe's same-period report for larger managed buildings shows a considerably higher average near $1,818. Ohio City units vary depending on whether it's an older rowhouse conversion or newer construction.

faq.q: What ZIP code is Ohio City in?
faq.a: Ohio City is in ZIP code 44113, on Cleveland's near-west side, directly across the Cuyahoga River from downtown.

faq.q: What transit is near 2-bedroom apartments in Ohio City?
faq.a: Ohio City has its own RTA Red Line station, connecting to downtown Tower City in one direction and Cleveland Hopkins Airport and the east side in the other. West 25th Street and I-90 are both close by.

faq.q: Do Ohio City landlords accept Section 8 vouchers for 2-bedroom units?
faq.a: It depends on the individual landlord. Cleveland, including Ohio City, has no ordinance requiring voucher acceptance, since Ohio has no statewide source-of-income law and the city hasn't passed its own. Every home Rent Finder Cleveland works with in the area accepts Section 8 vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready.
